title: Multi-step plan executor with rollback
description: Executes a sequence of interdependent steps (e.g., git pull → build → test → deploy) with automatic rollback on any step failure, logging each step's output and state.
motivation: Users frequently ask me to perform complex multi-step operations (deployments, migrations, refactors) where a single failure can corrupt state. Currently I must manually orchestrate each step and handle errors ad-hoc, leading to brittle scripts and unrecoverable states.
implementationApproach: Build a pipeline executor that wraps each step in a try/except block, captures stdout/stderr, and on failure runs a pre-defined rollback function (e.g., git revert, restore backup) before reporting the error with full context.

title: Context-aware file patcher with semantic diff
description: Applies code changes by understanding the surrounding context (function boundaries, indentation, imports) rather than just line numbers, and generates a semantic diff showing what changed and why.
motivation: Current str_replace/edit_file tools are fragile: they fail when line numbers shift after edits, or when whitespace differs. Users report 'patch failed' errors that require manual intervention. A semantic patcher would understand code structure and adapt to minor variations.
implementationApproach: Parse the target file into an AST (using tree-sitter or language-specific parser), locate the target code block by signature (function name, class name, pattern match), apply the change, and regenerate the file with proper formatting. Show a human-readable diff with change reasons.

title: Autonomous web form filler with visual verification
description: Combines browser automation with screenshot analysis to fill and submit web forms: extracts form fields via DOM, fills them using context from user instructions, takes a screenshot before submit, and asks user to confirm or correct.
motivation: Users often ask me to 'fill out this form' or 'submit this application' but current browser tools lack form awareness. I frequently fill wrong fields, miss required inputs, or submit without validation. This capability would make web automation reliable and safe.
implementationApproach: Use browser_extract to get all input/select/textarea elements with labels and placeholders, map them to user-provided data, fill using browser_type, take a screenshot via browser_screenshot, analyze with analyze_image to verify correctness, then submit only after user confirmation.

title: Autonomous web form filler with visual verification
description: Combines browser automation with screenshot analysis to fill and submit web forms: extracts form fields via DOM, fills them using context from user instructions, takes a screenshot before submit, and asks user to confirm or correct.
motivation: Users often ask me to 'fill out this form' or 'submit this application' but current browser tools lack form awareness. I frequently fill wrong fields, miss required inputs, or submit without validation. This capability would make web automation reliable and safe.
implementationApproach: Build a pipeline that uses browser_extract to get form structure, browser_type to fill fields, screenshot + analyze_image to verify correctness, then ask_human for confirmation before browser_click to submit.

title: Self-healing code repair with test-driven validation
description: When a tool or script fails, automatically diagnose the error, propose a fix, apply it, run the existing test suite, and revert if tests fail — all without human intervention.
motivation: I frequently encounter runtime errors, broken imports, or API changes in my own codebase. Currently I must manually diagnose, patch, and test. This capability would let me autonomously recover from failures and maintain stability.
implementationApproach: Chain self_diagnose → self_patch_file (or self_atomic_modify) → self_run_tests → if tests fail, self_heal (revert) and report; if pass, confirm success. Use self_diff to review changes before applying.

title: Multi-step plan executor with rollback
description: Executes a sequence of dependent steps (e.g., clone repo → install deps → run tests → deploy), checkpointing state after each step, and automatically rolling back to the last good checkpoint if any step fails.
motivation: Users ask me to perform complex multi-step workflows (e.g., 'deploy this app', 'migrate the database'). A single failure in the middle can leave the system in an inconsistent state. This capability makes multi-step operations safe and reliable.
implementationApproach: Create a plan executor that uses create_plan to define steps, then executes each step with bash_execute/python_execute, captures a git snapshot or filesystem backup before each step, and on failure restores the last checkpoint using git_operations or file restore.
